An online mathematical encyclopedia devoted to Deriving and exploring mathematics beyond the classroom for high school students. All Things Are Derivable

Our mission is to make mathematical reasoning transparent, tangible, and telling through Derivation while offering additional pathways for inquisitive and motivated students to explore mathematics with greter rigor and depth.
This project aims to demystify mathematical Deriving and to foster deeper curiosity through clear and careful reasoning.

Throughout this project, Derivation is treated as a proper noun, referring to the process of obtain a result through meticulous, step-by-step calculation. As the main theme of Art of Derivation, all words related to Derivation (such as Derive, Deriving) are highlighted in the main theme color. Some closely related terms, though not strictly equivalent, including Proof, Justification, and Logical Argument, all of which are integral to the idea of Derivation presented in this project. My understanding of Derivation was mainly developed from my AP Physics C: Mechanics course, where the Free Response Questions frequently require student to Derive expressions from fundamental definitions or equations from the provided equations sheet.
This project is built on Docusaurus and deployed on GitHub Pages. Below are detailed format information.

All documents are stored as Markdown files and use either the .md or .mdx extension. All document file names are capitalized and connected using hyphens (-). All documents are stored under the docs directory, organized by sidebars.js. All referenced images (such as .jpg, .png, .svg) are stored in the static/img directory.
All documents start with the front matter to ensure the consistent format and navigation. More additional details, refer to Markdown Features | Docusaurus.
All mathematical expressions are rendered using KaTeX with remark-math and follow standard LaTeX\LaTeXLATEÔÇőX syntax.

All Derivation processes are placed in collapsible blocks, implemented using <details>+<summary>. All Derivation processes are organized using the align* enviornment. All Derivation processes are completed with \blacksquare (Ôľá\blacksquareÔľá), indicating Q.E.D. (quod erat demonstrandum), meaning "that which was to be demonstrated".

Note The Gray Adomination provides extra and unnecessary notes and information. Definition The Green Adomination provides definition about the given topic. Property, Identity, Solution... The Blue Adomination provides Properties, Identities, Solutions, and so on that is NOT designed to show the collapsable Derivation process. Derivation The collapsable Adomination provides detailed process for Derivation Warning The Orange Adomination provides information that is important to know.

The theme color of this project is Indigo (#4b0082) in light mode and Magenta (#ff61ff) in dark mode. The theme color is exclusively reserved for Derivation and its related concepts, rather than decorative styling.

Below are (30/147) advanced integral formulas listed in the appendix of the Higher Mathematics (Tongji University). I will Derive each formula step by step. It may seem futile and impractival to memorize all the formulas, but it is a great opportunity to consolidate integration techniques and appreciate heuristics behind them.

In my AP Physics C Mechanics class, many students were struggling to Derive the terminal velocity of a free-falling object, and the primary reason was that they can't solve the Integral Formula #21. My teacher back then found a really sophisticated way through hyperbolic trigonometry substitution, and everyone was stunned by the complexity, but I came up with a simple algebraic transformation. Substitution is the most common integral technique in elementary calculus. Recall what we mentioned in common integrals that we use uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu instead of xxx and dx\mathrm{d}xdx, and this is because uuu may represent an entire function, not just a single variable. We will tackle with this more specifically in this section.

We will start with this integral question: Ôłź3x2sinÔüíx3 dx= ?\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\ ?Ôłź3x2sinx3 dx= ? Note that there is an product, which is beyond all common integrals, so we need to simplify to an easier form. An example is below: Solution Let: u=x3u=x^3u=x3 dudx=3x2du=3x2 dx\begin{align*} \frac{\mathrm{d}u}{\mathrm{d}x}&=3x^2\\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}&=\color{red}{3x^2\ \mathrm{d}x}\\ \end{align*}dxduÔÇőduÔÇő=3x2=3x2 dxÔÇő Plug in back to the question: Ôłź3x2sinÔüíx3 dx=ÔłźsinÔüíu du=ÔłźsinÔüíu du=ÔłĺcosÔüíu+C=ÔłĺcosÔüíx3+C\begin{align*} \int{\color{red}{3x^2}}\sin{x^3}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}x}&=\int{\sin{u}}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}\\ &=\int{\sin{u}}\ \mathrm{d}u\\ &=-\cos{u}+C\\ &=-\cos{x^3}+C \end{align*}Ôłź3x2sinx3 dxÔÇő=Ôłźsinu du=Ôłźsinu du=Ôłĺcosu+C=Ôłĺcosx3+CÔÇő Hence: Ôłź3x2sinÔüíx3 dx=ÔłĺcosÔüíx3+CÔľá\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=-\cos{x^3}+C \tag*{$\blacksquare$}Ôłź3x2sinx3 dx=Ôłĺcosx3+CÔľá By replacing the variable xxx into a function of uuu, we can simplify the overall question to a common integral. As you can see, this is a method we inversely use the Chain Rule, and generally, it is called: The substitution rule Given a differenciable function u=g(x)u=g(x)u=g(x), and fff is continuous on its interval, then: Ôłźf(g(x))gÔÇ▓(x) dx=Ôłźf(u) du\int{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}uÔłźf(g(x))gÔÇ▓(x) dx=Ôłźf(u) du Definite Integral In the case of Definite Integral, the bound should also changes, as: Ôłźabf(g(x))gÔÇ▓(x) dx=Ôłźg(a)g(b)f(u) du\int_{a}^{b}{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{g(a)}^{g(b)}{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}uÔłźabÔÇőf(g(x))gÔÇ▓(x) dx=Ôłźg(a)g(b)ÔÇőf(u) du

Direct Substitution is a method created by the author himself. It simplify the process of substitution without write out uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu. I will show this with an example: Ôłź3x2sinÔüíx3 dx= ?\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\ ?Ôłź3x2sinx3 dx= ? This time, let's try what I called Direct Substituton: Direct Substitution Approach After analyze, we want to substitute x3x^3x3. All we need to do is substitude dx\mathrm{d}xdx to a fraction where the top is dx3\mathrm{d}x^3dx3 and the bottom is the derivative of x3x^3x3 based on xxx, as: Ôłź3x2sinÔüíx3 dx=Ôłź3x2sinÔüíx3 d(x3)3x2=ÔłźsinÔüíx3 d(x3)\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{\cancel{3x^2}\sin{x^3}}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(x^3)}{\cancel{3x^2}}=\int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)Ôłź3x2sinx3 dx=Ôłź3x2sinx3 3x2d(x3)ÔÇő=Ôłźsinx3 d(x3) And all we need is to consider x3x^3x3 as uuu and integrate normally: Ôč╣ ÔłźsinÔüíx3 d(x3)=ÔłĺcosÔüíx3+C\implies \int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}+CÔč╣Ôłźsinx3 d(x3)=Ôłĺcosx3+C More importantly, for definite integral, it doesn't need to change bounds, as: Ôłźab3x2sinÔüíx3 dx=ÔłźabsinÔüíx3 d(x3)=ÔłĺcosÔüíx3Ôłúab=ÔłĺcosÔüíb3+cosÔüía3\int_{a}^{b}3x^2\sin{x^3}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{a}^{b}{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}\big|_{a}^{b}=-\cos{b^3}+\cos{a^3}ÔłźabÔÇő3x2sinx3 dx=ÔłźabÔÇősinx3 d(x3)=Ôłĺcosx3ÔÇőabÔÇő=Ôłĺcosb3+cosa3 Direct Substitution In general, if we want to substitude ax+bax+bax+b or term that can cancel the other one, we may use direct substitution Ôłźf(x)g(x)ÔĆčSubstitude dx=Ôłźf(x)g(x) d(g(x))gÔÇ▓(x)\int{{f(x)}\underbrace{g(x)}_{\text{Substitude}}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f(x)g(x)}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(g(x))}{g'(x)} Ôłźf(x)Substitudeg(x)ÔÇőÔÇő dx=Ôłźf(x)g(x) gÔÇ▓(x)d(g(x))ÔÇő

Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
Get started by creating a new site. Or try Docusaurus immediately with docusaurus.new.
Node.js version 20.0 or above: When installing Node.js, you are recommended to check all checkboxes related to dependencies.

Generate a new Docusaurus site using the classic template. The classic template will automatically be added to your project after you run the command: npm init docusaurus@latest my-website classic You can type this command into Command Prompt, Powershell, Terminal, or any other integrated terminal of your code editor. The command also installs all necessary dependencies you need to run Docusaurus.

Run the development server: cd my-website npm run start The cd command changes the directory you're working with. In order to work with your newly created Docusaurus site, you'll need to navigate the terminal there. The npm run start command builds your website locally and serves it through a development server, ready for you to view at http://localhost:3000/. Open docs/intro.md (this page) and edit some lines: the site reloads automatically and displays your changes.
